channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 116 replies — 186.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 116 • Updated: 2026-06-28 04:17 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62414680
>>62414279
If you do that, its boomer tier analysis, otherwise that analysis would dictate to just buy VOO or QQQ overlooking the real value of VT/I. VT is used as a hedge if there's an exodus from U.S. markets in the next 5-10 years. VTI is used as a hedge against an exodus from bloated tech in the next 5-10 years but the U.S is still supreme (likely the case). When investment eventually settles from AI bloat and only 3-5 companies out of the current hundreds trying to pursue AI development come out on top, investment will switch into a different sector. With these etfs, you dont have to worry about what the next big thing is, which is why its the ultimate hold. Zero think. Comfy returns and they trail VOO by total return over 10 years 309% vs 325%, VT and VTI both have lowe p/e which makes the more value and less risk. Buy, hold, DRIP, and sell when you want to. This is the long end of my portfolio, and my 401k and IRAs. This is shit I am not touching for 30-40 years. Some people in my firm freak out over market turmoil, im zen, where one bucket empties, another fills for me.

62414768
>>62413705
>Felix and friends
pic is the better Felix

62414776
>>62414162
vti+vxus is more tax efficient

62414829
>>62414776
Barely, but VXUS does have the pas-through tax credit, something that amounts to $100 per $100k invested, which is better, plus you can set your parameters for how much you want allocated to international. But have you ever had to explain to a 24yo HR roastie to set up 15% portfolio allocation of VTIAX/VXUS?
>HALF HERE HALF THERE IT CANT BE THIS FUCKING HARD
The headache is not worth the hassle.

62415002
>>62410414
VOO is not 15%, its 10%
>since inception
I don't give a shit, its such a low sample that it's nearly statistically irrelevant. Here is that data from 1900-2016. US Large is VOO

62416552
Damn Breh just got a $2200 check do I just invest it boringly or do something fun with it

62417956
>>62416552
sweet

62418163
>>62414680
That's actually super comfy anon. I think I'll set up an account like that.

62419099
>ytd, 1 yr, 3 yr, 5 yr
My gains from picking a portfolio of 40-70 value stocks, ETFs, maybe 10% of portfolio in growth stocks which I cycle into something else whenever they run up a lot

I normally would agree with op but there’s a lot of research saying likely forward returns at these S&P500 multiples are going to be less than 2% a year

62419144
>>62401790
Perplexity Finance used several methodologies and concluded $1m in 2026 usd was approximately the entry level wealth for the equestrian class. Interesting

62419159
>>62409811
This. You should spend time and energy to increase your income. That will boost your savings rate.

62419709
>>62419099
What have you picked and what’s your philosophy
The reason I have been doing S&P ETFs is that it’s simple and I was just starting out
I basically just put my entire paycheck in there a week so it became an easy 2 min habit using an app

62419913
>>62419709
I follow smart people on x and read 13f statements from great investors. No grifters or gurus or people with mental illnesses, narcissism, or neuroses. Example: Cluseau Investments. This gives me most of my ideas these days because I've become a bit more lazy. I used to run screens on various sites for at least 10 hours a week. I also downloaded the entire stock ticker universe and then enriched the data with finnhub, Alpha Vantage, etc. I even did completely mong shit like having a python script take a screenshot of every known stock loaded into eval sites like GuruFocus etc and then simply counting the green pixel ratio.
But like I said I found I could just dumb this down to leech ideas from smart people who have already done this work.

99% of my buys need a PEG ratio below 1.2, forward p/e preferably cheap like below 13, and growth of both revenue and profit has to be up and to the right. Price to sales I'd also like to be low (I won't own spacex due to this).

Then I consider whether I think it will do well due to sector and if it's got a long base where it's gone nowhere. I like long bases where it's fallen out of favor because generally, everything will have its day again, plus I already protected myself quite a bit on the downside by needing low PEG ratios (either p/e is very low compared to growth or growth is very high compared to p/e). The only thing that can turn it into a disaster from this POV is if projected earnings are wildly wrong, or there is some kind of danger that turns a cheap 7 p/e stock into an absurdly cheap 3 p/e stock (those exist. I even own a 0.75 p/e serial acquirer from Germany that I lost $6k on). But I can afford a bunch of disasters per year because I have a big portfolio where I punt around 0.4% to 1.5% on each idea.

62419916
>>62419709
>>62419913
>continued due to length

>but what about ROIC, SBC, f-score, z-score etc
I used to meticulously track those too and avoid anything spooky, and I got good results. But now my final heuristic before pressing the order button is generally searching the ticker I want to buy on social media and seeing what other people see as particularly attractive or dangerous. If the stock has high SBC (stock based compensation) someone will always chime up about it and I avoid it

I've probably forgotten something, but tl;dr I've attacked stock selection from every angle and now I feel like I can just feel it out very quickly based on a couple dumb heuristics. If you have to do pretzel math to convince yourself the stock is a good idea, it's a worse idea than the good ideas you'll come across so why buy it.

62419932
>>62419709
>>62419916
That said, I also pile up a bunch of ETFs for additional diversification. People hate on diversification these days, and even Warren Buffett says derisively that diversification is protection from ignorance. I’m happy with that. Win in my book

62419947
What about things like DRAM and SOXX
Especially SOXX since it invests in the entirety of the AI infrastructure, wouldn't that be a big bet vs just going into the market

62419965
>>62419947
Semiconductors as a sector is basically guaranteed up and to the right over the long term but look at the chart right now. It would be completely unsurprising IF people who buy here after this insane pump are stuck in a negative position for many years, even a decade. Happens a lot

62419969
>>62419965
Yeah...I keep missing the boat lol.....and now it seems like Apple is asking to buy chinese memory chips so I wonder if Im lucky to not be in micron rn

62419980
By the way guys, here's a cool free tool to backtest ETF compositions:
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com/backtest-portfolio#analysisResults

I forgot the exact ratio but I read that tech plus small caps combo is gold tier returns wise and it seemed to be true when I last played with this tool.

>>62419969
If you get frustrated it's best to just buy the whole market imho. You get better returns than most people (average retail investor only gets around 5% CAGR) and get to keep your time and peace of mind

62419989
>>62419980
im fine with doing this shit long term but do have slight fomo about all this stuff being a once in a lifetime money making opportunity....but i guess if that were true the residual "go up and right" should also last a while

62420075
>negative correlation between amount of text in a post and intelligence of that post

62420076
>>62420075
Youre gay lol

62420079
>>62420076
no u

62420460
>>62420079
owned

62420887
>>62420075
Brevity is the soul of wit.

62421697
Anyone here going into DRAM

62421773
VOO and chill is the best because it's the most safe/leaning aggressive fund you can get. Alternatives:
>individual stocks: this always looks great in hindsight, but who the fuck knew that Micron was going to skyrocket? And if you picked Microsoft you would be underperforming VOO
>volatile ETFs like BAKA, DRAM, TQQQ: these are great when the market is up, but when you lose 15% in a few days you will feel it
>BTC, ETH: yeah right, the S&P beats these easily (inb4 "zoom out", nobody cares, that kind of increase will never be duplicated)

62421787
>>62420887
Brevity: Wit's soul

62421799
>"just VOO and chill bro"
>gets caught in a 5+ yr -60% drawdown event (2008) with no hedges to cover his balls
boyhood is regurgitating the VOO mantra
adulthood is meticulously engineering your poorfolio to get within 80% of index performance while cutting the drawdown periods by half

62421813
>>62400493
Or I can throw a good amount into Uranium miner indexes and chill to retire in half the time

item.title: how the FUCK do you get a job in this economy? — 41 replies — 134.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
how the FUCK do you get a job in this economy?

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-06-28 04:36 UTC

OP

how the FUCK do you get a job in this economy?

Early replies (first 3)

62417182
know somebody

62417183
firm handshake

62417184
become a plumber

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62418538
literally just get an entry level job at a big company like a bank and work your way up

I was a dropout neet in 2016 and I got a job at a mail room in a bank office part time making minimum wage. Made some moves up and now I'm a """"cyber security analyst"""" which is a bullshit laptop job.

62418632
>>62417275
ive never had a job in 10 years, i show up to interviews, awkwardly go through it, never hear back. I gave up desu. I have no idea how people get jobs or stay in them without burning out.

62418762
>>62417275
Yeah honestly it’s not hard, I have gone through 3 jobs in the last five years,
The unemployment rate is low too so it’s not like they have an excuse

Maybe they like just graduated high school or something

62418768
>>62417164
I work the opposite side. I'm an independent contractor, businesses apply to me to get me to work for them. I interview them and check their references and if they make the cut I'll go work for them. I have far more applications than I can ever work for, so it's never a problem finding work

62418796
>>62417270
Based. Postman is a close second
T. Postman

62418819
>>62417182
>>62417183
>>62417183
Do all of these one after another OP

62418886
>>62417275
Not finishing hs and being terminally unhireable with no contacts and demoralized for 2 decades will do it

62418899
>>62418796
Honestly my ideal job...being paid to walk. I also dont care about keeping it more than 5 years so im willing to fuck up someones dog if it came to that

62418900
i hate normies who just talk casually about how easy they get jobs. you people are monkeys. GIVE ME A JOB IF ITS SO EASY

62418901
>>62418900
Well nepotism still exist.

62418926
>>62418538
>literally just get an entry level job at a big company like a bank and work your way up
Is this before or after I give a firm handshake and hand them my resume?

62419033
>>62417184
Tick tock, tradie.

https://www.anthropic.com/research/project-fetch-phase-two

62419102
>>62418886
Bro, get your GED. And then go to trade school or something. You managed to get to high school, all you gotta do is finish. Getting hired in this era of extreme nepotism, on the other hand, I don't know. It's likely all online job listings are fake, as I was never hired through those (I have an actual email from a so-called Mary Perez from Burlington gloating about it them being fake). I just about managed to get hired at an ISP during a job fair after completing a networking certificate. All I had to do was pass their written test. Though, you really need to work your way asap towards a CCNA if you want to keep the job. Just need to follow Comptia's cert map. You could also go for trades like Electrician or Plumbing, which could lead to self-employment, but I can't help you with those.

62419300
>>62417164
nurses and care takers are important and needed.

office chair warming keyboard clackers are getting culled.

the world is healing

62419584
be good at what you do
im so good at what i do they gave me a car to get there and a paid commute because initially turned down the job because i didnt want to drive that far

62419594
>>62417164
You take what's being offered and figure it out from there. Get a factory job, a Walmart job, etc.

62419800
>>62418899
I'm approaching year 4 and I've only had two dog incidents. It's always the small dogs. Big dogs may bark if their owner isn't around but odds are they are well trained and you can tell them to sit and they listen. The supervisors just tells us not to even attempt delivery if there are dogs in a yard because they don't want to do paperwork for incidents. Also mixed feelings on this, my area is converting to community mailboxes so pretty much eliminating walking. I like driving around and it's super easy to deliver to one spot but I'll have to start doing more cardiovascular exercise outside of work.

62419806
>>62417164
Put nigger on your CV. Under experiences write police brutality. The femoids in HR will pay you to go work there.

62420387
>>62418796
I'm a train driver and feel like you guys work harder. Would love to be a postman on a postie bike if trains get automated here.

62420535
>>62419102
>It's likely all online job listings are fake
At least 90% are and the few remaining get hundreds of applicants so you're guaranteed to be competing with PHDs for 18 bucks an hour work.

62420580
>>62417275
Dont want to discourage anyone, maybe its just me.
i have 10 years in aerospace eng., took me 16 months to find a job. Have sent thousands of applications, hundreds of linkedin dms. Even some cold calls (actually cold calling worked best). Granted the new job is FAANG but requires relocation.

62420599
>>62417164
Be brown and pay an Indian college for a fake degree. You might be able to get a job at a Walmart then.

62420653
>>62418900
i think a big part of the sub culture of 4chan is introverts. you have to talk to people, like in real life. getting a mcjob trade job is a lot easier these days, getting a decent tech job not so much.

otherwise fake being an incompetent LGBTWFTBBQ, big companies love that shit

62420692
This is the new normal, I fear.

62420767
>>62417182
Truke

62420776
>>62417275
>I’m honestly confused how you guys have so much trouble.
14 years gap in my resume, it's the kind of gap you can't come back from

62421717
>>62417164
You don't. You just stay in the basement till your parents lose it all then move to skidrow.

62421753
>>62417164
backstory behind OPs pic?

62421810
>>62421753
imagine working and a nigger messes with you

62421837
>>62420387
Here in Western Canada, we still have trapped time in our contract. I can either get my 8 hour day done in 3 hours and leave or do overtime without actually physically working more than 8 hours.

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 96 replies — 108.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 96 • Updated: 2026-06-28 04:02 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62414950
>>62413082
It won't work forever

62414955
>>62414944
How?

62415214
>>62414950
Hmmm, that's why it's still in public testnet instead of being pushed straight into production. I'm more interested in where it could lead than writing it off this early.

62415217
>>62414893
MSTR has definitely been a major buyer, but I don't think Bitcoin's entire value proposition depends on one company continuing to buy forever.

62415228
>>62395655
Because I have no brown, black or asian admixture.

62415296
>>62395655
Bitcorn has no liquidity now - there are no baggies left to trap.

I'd rather gamble on stocks - at least the price actually moves.

62415297
>>62396879
There is 'demand' for literally meme garbage like PEPE and DOGE which do nothing - not really an argument.

62415301
>>62415217
No, but MSTR collapse will cause a negative doom loop for Bitcorn if they have to start dumping.

62415492
>>62415228
Are you white?

62416132
>>62395655
I sold between 90-110k
Bought back between 59-64k
Still 1/3 of Tether left to buy further dips

62416334
>>62411213
Yeah. The Aegis partnership makes me even more interested to see how the TBV public testnet progresses.

62416338
>>62415301
but short term

62416355
>>62416132
Nice trades.

62416385
>>62395655
because I make money trading not holding garbage

62416439
>>62416385
Because I believe Bitcoin is the strongest long-term store of value in crypto. Short-term volatility doesn't change why I bought it in the first place.

62416488
>>62411265
I'm not holding bitcoin for next week. I'm holding it for the next decade.

62416497
>>62411213
I have doubts for Aave coin

62416506
Because every major dip has looked scary in the moment, but the long-term trend is what matters to me.

62416528
eistein coin

62417470
>>62416385
Garbage? Seriously?

62417484
>>62416497
Nobody's saying you have to like the coin. The point is the protocol and why it still dominates DeFi lending.

62417496
>>62411241
your choice

62417853
>>62415297
Memecoins are different

62419146
>>62395655
Because I'm not a retard

62419150
I know the maxis wont want to watch this…but btc is fundamentally flawed, as are all pow and pos consensus mechanisms…it’s not gonna work long term
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=fo8ZScrXFZE&ra=m
Jesus loves you, Christ is King

62419226
>>62395655
I dont hold anything with zero value...maybe trade it

62419255
>>62419226
That's funny. From $1 to $100k and zero value?

62419275
>>62419255
Zero value as in speculative/doesnt generate a cashflow...sorry you know nothing about finance.

62420185
>>62419146
Non coiner. Ok

62421786
>>62419150
Hmm, that's your opinion.

item.title: Another stablecoin just RUGGED — 41 replies — 41.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Another stablecoin just RUGGED

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-06-28 03:55 UTC

OP

MIM (Magic Internet Money) stablecoin just rugged.
So many stablecoins are rugging right now it's crazy

Early replies (first 3)

62408562
IT has just begun

62408566
magic internet money still exists?

62408598
>>62408557
amazing they're even still around

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62414658
>>62414655
I'm actually amazed they were still running this and never caught any legal flak for the role they played in the terraluna ponzi scheme and collapse

62414664
this one MIGHT be a slurper
hold on

62414718
>>62414664
The shit people will buy instead of Bitcoin. Just lmfao. Sad really.

62414728
so depending on the collat that they start hiking rates on... this might re-peg
might

62414746
most likely won't re-peg
>reason:
their depreciated cauldrons is where most of the dogshit collat likely is
even if liquidated, they might not cover for a full repeg

62414751
>>62414664
Why would I want to repay my debt?

62414752
>>62414655
>manually approve more emissions via multisig
LMAO

62414758
>>62414751
dudes who borrowed from this might be better off letting liquidation happen than getting their dogshit collat back
only God knows what fuckery might be in the "depreciated cauldrons"

62414805
>>62414664
I will not be buying any but if you buy and it repairs, sell it the second it does.
>>62414718
>Buying bitcoin
Lmfao Buy real coins with actual utility.

62414828
>>62414805
I won't buy, this shit has thin liquidity
people might panic and send it to fractions of a cent just for the fuck of it
of course, at that point everyone is going to crowd and buy
but right now, it just looks like something went structurally wrong
and by what that guy posted, considering this shit is managed by fucking multisigs that put rates manually into the contracts...
it kinda does

62414831
>>62414805
>if you buy and it repairs

How’s the weather in Bangladesh?

62414841
>>62414831
Reread what I wrote retard. I said I will not buy it but encouraged him to not baghold the exit repeg if it happens because it never recovers and stays. You are so cooked by doom scrolling reels it is insane. You are incapable of reading a single sentence with patience for comprehension.

62414846
>>62414828
Maybe it will have a bounce somewhere along the line like UST did but do not bag hold it hoping for complete par recovery. Look at the LUNC generals in the archive if you want to feel the blood drain from your face imagining being them (and link is being outperformed by fucking LUNC, if you want to REALLY imagine being a bagholder of the deepest kind lol).

62414884
Why are you retards still in crypto??

62415000
>>62408557
usdc and tether can also depeg, then it's truly over
they can kill crypto at any time they want, wouldn't take more than the epstein class seizing circle and tether or their reserves
their buddies would all sell first, but this is a card they can and will play eventually if the goyim get too powerful and you will lose everything

62416138
>>62414841
>>62414846
Nice double post, yeah it takes about two seconds of reading your gibberish (which no one else will ever reply to because you’re brown and your opinion doesn’t matter) to tell you’re ESL as fuck and should GTFO

62416716
>>62415000
>usdc and tether can also depeg
they can only market depeg
their collat is cash equivalent, literally impossible to depeg unless the US government ceases to exist

62416862
>>62414658
Did the people behind MIM play a role in that? I thought MIM was just used (among several other stablecoins) as an attack vector on Curve to de-peg UST. Which we later found out (through a lawsuit) was allegedly done by Jane Street.

Unless I'm missing something then blaming the MIM creators for it is the same as blaming the USDT and USDC creators, which makes no sense

62416965
>>62416862
This is the MIM marketcap. It spiked this high because of the UST "degenbox" strategy that took advantage of Terra's fixed high yields. See that first blip in January 2022? That was in reaction to the collapse of the Wonderland (TIME) ponzi scheme, which was run by the same people as MIM. This was the beginning of the end for Terra, as liquidity rapidly pulled out, Do Kwon would then make a fatal error when he failed to understand that billions of dollars could be withdrawn on a hair trigger. Is MIM solely responsible for Terraluna's collapse? No... but they were definitely in the thick of it

62417049
>>62416862
>Did the people behind MIM play a role in that?
daniele does not have the brains for that
he's mostly a vibes guy

62417050
>>62414664
>across all Cauldrons

lmao imagine buying this shit ever

62418454
>>62418339
it's a fucking financial market
if you want funny names go play monopoly

62419082
>>62415000
They can only depeg if the government collapses on their bonds, becauae thats where they park the usds they get for minting their coins. Any stablecoin that offers more than 3% is basically lending it out to someone which carries enormous risk if it doesnt get repaid.

62419307
I'm collecting these ruggpull screenshots.
It's a hobby of mine.
I'm very excited for the future...

62419313
>>62408557
imagine filing bankruptcy because you lost all your money on something named Magic Internet Money

62419523
>>62419313
>>62419313

62419557
next in line: chainlink

62421752
day 16 of depegging

62421775
figures crypto faggots are into "pegging"

LOL BAGGIES
LOL KEKS

good luck with your cauldrons of magic money or whatever the fuck you retards are doing

item.title: What's the fundamental bull case for this? — 28 replies — 40.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
What's the fundamental bull case for this?

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-06-28 04:13 UTC

OP

What's the fundamental bull case for this? What about USDT/USDC adoption? 2.5% APR seems nice too.
It's pretty cheap right now and I'm considering buying some.

Early replies (first 3)

62420784
>>62420744
it's all funny money, anon.

62420787
The bull case for the past 4-5 years has been that institutions will utilize ETH for the tokenization of assets like stocks.

Well, turns out the institutions decided to use ETH as a blueprint to create their own private smart contract platforms, while picking and choosing which features they want to keep. Their view of ETH is merely that of a public testing grounds for what works and what doesn't. It's grim

62420791
>>62420787
Damn

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62420794
>>62420791
Before you buy any, ask yourself why so many people from the Ethereum Foundation has been jumping ship recently. If the future was bright, would they do that?

62420797
Yall gots dat efferiumz?

62420858
>>62420787
>private smart contract platforms
If you don't see the value of a neutral and decentralized chain like Ethereum, you don't understand the basics of crypto. Why do you think there exists only one internet and not multiple with different corpos/govs in control?

62420859
>>62420787
what is decentralization, biz?

62420863
>>62420858
its biz, what did u expect?

62420871
>>62420863
>crypto board doesn't understand crypto
Makes sense

62420875
>>62420859
Something institutions don't really seem to give a fuck about. They're in love with smart contracts and tokenization, both of which can be used on centralized platforms. They don't give a single shit about decentralization

62420884
>>62420858
I see the value in it. I thought institutions would too. That's why I held ETH.

Turns out the institutions don't see the value in it

62420891
Well, consider that historically there have been three, and only three, crypto use cases big enough to attract significant numbers of mainstream people that aren't in it for speculation or ideology. And two of those use cases, stablecoins and gambling, are currently dominated by Ethereum, with no real signs of that changing.
Say what you want about what those use cases says about crypto as a whole, but it does show that Ethereum is the most genuinely attractive chain, which is a bull case.

(By the way, the third use case, dark markets, are dominated by privacy coins like Monero)

62420908
>>62420875
and I dont give a fuck about your institution excel sheets(ticker:blockchains)

62420913
>>62420871
nigger board does not understand anything, buy pregnant butt saar!>>624

62420918
>>62420884
who would have thought when institutions(leeches) stepping in they willl want all the pesos to leech out from it.... have fun buying 4% circulated supply institutional private chain bags!

62420920
>>62420918
the 4% stands for spacex for example

62420952
>>62420908
>>62420913
>>62420918
>>62420920
Least insane mETH head

62420959
>>62420859
>>62420858
here is your descentrelized blockchain bro

62420963
>>62420952
thanks dood I just boat today 1.3 more eth

62420969
>>62420959
wow some rando nigger claimed usdt from a cex! ohnoes I just sold all my efferumz! UNSTAKING ATM BOIS!

62421452
>>62420787
Well the clueless institutions are trying that.

What they dont realise is, they already have a centralised controlled platform now. They just need to open the markets 24-7, and stop stupid 1950s nightly clearing house nonsense.

Instead they decided to make their own single data centre centralised shitcoin for some reason.

It's laughable.

The reason to use Eth as the backbone is it is by far the most decentralized crypto capable of supporting complex finance.
You can't run a stock market on Solana as it's literally a centralised shitcoin running on datacenters.

The eth chain is running on random PCs everywhere. Much harder to attack.

62421507
>>62420952
sad to see, he's gone schizo!

62421508
>>62420794
Most are not leaving the ecosystem, just the foundation. With its new limited scope those interested in other areas will form new orgs

62421559
>>62420744
ETH made me a millionaire. Only SOL shilling pajeets go against it. Buy some ETH and stake it. It's literally free money.

62421748
>>62421452
institutions dont give a crap about this, they been centralized since forever and there is no reason to put any ETH on their balancesheets. the ROI is shitty, the tech is trash, only autists use it for scams and it will never be actually mainstream.

62421764
>>62420787
>their own private smart contract platforms
lmao they didn't
you're delusional and completely out of the loop

62421794
>>62421748
>institutions
stop with the schizo talk
nobody is making a fucking geth clone to run on JPM lmao
bagholder talk goes both ways
the retards saying shit like "bofa will use aave for their deposits " are the same level of retard on the niggers saying "private blockchains"
there is no reason for private blockchains, it's a donkey pulling a Ferrari

62421807
before I forget
>>62420744
>What's the fundamental bull case for this?
to use the VM, you must order transactions, specially when interacting with liquidity
ordering is more important in a turing complete virtual machine chain than on a UTXO one, in fact
the only way for a blockchain to properly secure ordering is with its native block reward, nothing else works

buying ether as an investment means you believe that onchain activity will grow, both in volume and in value
pic related is a moron who doesn't understand how blockchains work, and specially, how the blockchain secures his stablecoin product from going to literally 0 through double-spends

62414074
>>62414034
>But what does XRP even do?
It has US gov approved stablecoin , and it's a protocol of trading for banks.

Are you banned in Google?
>xrp news.
>ripple news
https://ripple.com/press-releases/
https://ripple.com/ripple-press/ripple-to-acquire-rail-for-200m-expanding-leadership-in-stablecoin-payments/
>Ripple buys 40-year-old firm GTreasury in $1 billion deal
https://www.thestreet.com/crypto/ripple-buys-40-year-old-firm-in-1-billion-deal

62414113
>>62414074
>US gov approved
So literal cancer? I bet trump coin is "us gov approved"

62414197
>>62412540
>>62412469
>>62414113
Since Trump revived crypto, XRP despite the marketwide selloff is up over 100% and crushing all other crypto, most of which is down. The spot ETFs launched exactly when the market turned over and it still was the most successful launch of any ETF product (same for CME futures) of the year despite launching initially on relatively small issuers. Since Trump revived crypto, LINK is underperforming fucking LUNC. Lol Literally all of xsg's average is .5 maxixum and we have been anticipating and mythologizing and hoping for a marketwide liquidation like this based on exactly these reasons (particularly Japan) and have been hoping for it for that same length of time. I am still up 6x on my average but would love a last buying opportunity pre-utility pricing, particularly in the context of a liquidity crisis, as we have suspected would happen. link is down literally over 90% from its ATH 6 years ago with zero prospect to return or at real profitability, unable to support 30% of its own network function, while Sirgay dumps on you all, and it roundtrips back to 6 dollars while literally every link holder here's average is bought at about $15 where it crabbed for years, with all of early biz having bought and sold in 2018/2019 when it was interesting before they signaled they would do nothing for token holders except for fundraise on them using headlines of "partnerships" that mean nothing for holders, with the only utililty of the token being a jewish rent seeking fee injection for no reason into a product that is slower and costlier and requires more trust than competitive products. No Love Deep Cope.

62414371
>>62412469
i bought at 2.40 dammit

62414390
XRP is now a USD stablecoin! congrats xrpbros!! BQQQQQQQM

62414411
>>62414390

62414425
>>62414411

The spot ETFs launched exactly when the market turned over and it still was the most successful launch of any ETF product (same for CME futures) of the year despite launching initially on relatively small issuers. I am still up 6x on my average but would love a last buying opportunity pre-utility pricing, particularly in the context of a liquidity crisis, as we have suspected would happen. link is down literally over 90% from its ATH 6 years ago with zero prospect to return or at real profitability, unable to support 30% of its own network function, while Sirgay dumps on you all, and it roundtrips back to 6 dollars while literally every link holder here's average is bought at about $15 where it crabbed for years, with all of early biz having bought and sold in 2018/2019 when it was interesting before they signaled they would do nothing for token holders except for fundraise on them using headlines of "partnerships" that mean nothing for holders, with the only utililty of the token being a jewish rent seeking fee injection for no reason into a product that is slower and costlier and requires more trust than competitive products. 62415477
>>62414764
You are a fucking faggot and a nigger. You can't win an argument and know your position is literally so indefensible you cannot be seen as reasonable or even remotely worth listening to if you engage from the base of it so you play extremely faggoted and jewish rhetoric games indistinguishable from how leftists engage online in rhetoric to try to paper over the material facts about your investment being a failure in price and having ZERO technical basis to recover or even justify itself at current price without being able to cover even 30% of network operation through revenues, hoping you will get to play pretend and will cognitive dissonance longer. You literally choose to not make it over just admitting you are wrong given a new marketwide price dump and opportunity to choose differently because you are a street shitting jeetnigger and a literal NPC of a greed cult that saw others get rich and bought their play after the ship sailed 7 years ago and the product never built to justify itself past the hype selloff originally. Unbelievably corny and gay. Being you is Hell.

62415920

62416491

62417965

62419081

62419412
>>62419081

item.title: They're completely fucked. GTAVI is the last major cash injection they will ever have. By the time they finish another g… — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
They're completely fucked. GTAVI is the last major cash injection they will ever have. By the time they finish another g…

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-28 04:50 UTC

OP

They're completely fucked. GTAVI is the last major cash injection they will ever have. By the time they finish another game of this scope, which could take ten years, AI will have completely and utterly commoditized every aspect of game development. Game publication will resemble book publication, with millions of titles published every year instead of 30k or so.

Early replies (first 3)

62421743
Gta online will continue being a cash cow and rdr 3 is going to be a thing

62421749
>>62421743
>GTA Online

500m a year and it's all downhill from here. Meanwhile the GTAVI release will make several billion. That's why I specified "cash injection".

>rdr3

All of Rockstar was focused on GTA. Development hasn't even started on RDR3. It will be years and years until release

62421750
>>62421715
And what stops Take Two from using the same AI that has you shitting your pantaloons?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62421757
>>62421715
>>62421749

Shut the fuck up retard.

Sincerely,

Real gamers.

62421761
>>62421750
It doesn't. But if you have countless GTA knockoffs, some of which become viral hits, Rockstar's moat is seriously diminished.

62421763
>>62421749
Also they still got gta4 remaster option.

62421771
>>62421715
>GTAVI
is this the wmlf simulator?

62421854
>>62421761
you severely underestimate brand power. the industry is already flooded with shovelware no one plays, AI accelerating this tenfold isn't going to make people play it either.

item.title: WE are the oil marines and we're not fucking selling — 17 replies — 21.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
WE are the oil marines and we're not fucking selling

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-28 04:08 UTC

OP

Another cargo ship just got blown up in the strait. They can't keep oil down forever.

Early replies (first 3)

62411834
>it’s true

62412180
I slurped some more oil

62413818
>we are the oil baggies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413900
you can sell now at 70
or at 40 when the Ukraine Russia war ends
your choice

62416450
>>62413900
Only a matter of time before reality hits the market. The emergency reserves are not infinite and it will take years to repair the damages in the gulf and in Russia.

62416754
>>62416450
They are actually 1 week away from losing pressure. Iran knows this and is in no rush.

62416764
>>62416450
This is doomer bullshit.

62417845
>>62416764
Look up direct quotes from gulf states about the amount of damage and how long it will take to go back to normal production. Even without the destruction, just the fact that they had to halt production means it will take months to reactivate facilities.
But who am I kidding, you won't look up shit because you are a stubborn low iq peak dunning kruger ass who won't change his mind anyway.

62417869
Oil reserves are the lowest that they have been in ~40 years. Even Sleepy Joe was chewing through reserves to suppress prices and that was without the turmoil in Iran.

The reserves have to be replenished. Even if the conflict ends tomorrow without any more turbulence, the damage has already been done. Some people buy a small position in oil as a hedge for their tech investments. If anything, I have a small position in tech as a hedge and most of my money in oil. Everyone will be crying come September and I will be sitting on fat stacks of cash courtesy of black gold while reminding them that I warned them.

62417932
Sell those bags when you get the chance, strikes happening now but idk how much more people will care

62417947
>>62416450
>it will take years to repair the damages in Russia.
So far, it has taken Russia less than a week to repair crude export terminals. Crude oil export volumes have not been affected.

Refinery damage is harder to see, however it's not nearly as much as the damage in the Gulf, because 50kg warheads simply do not cause much damage

62417949
>>62411825
The price is the price. It reflects a consensus amongst oil traders who are very sophisticated. Why do you think you possess knowledge that they don't? I think some oil stocks can be a good investment but derivatives on the commodities or ETFs that invest in them are a gamble.

62418308
>>62411825
The war hasnt ended and the "ceasefire" is fake and gay

expect $160 a barrel

62418377
Sure, you have the facts on your side. The bears have every world leader on their side, every industrial titan, trillions of dollars of economic output that all depends on oil staying affordable. You're a fool to challenge their ability to continue denying reality, they'll do whatever it takes to keep the numbers healthy.

62420386
>>62418377
That is literally what are those people already doing to keep the price under $100. After a certain point though, they won't be able to hold on anymore.

62421774
>>62413818
>oil barrelies

62421795
>>62416450
>he thinks the real reserves are openly communicated and updated

item.title: AI Related ETFs and Fields - Not Missing The Boat — 9 replies — 7.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI Related ETFs and Fields - Not Missing The Boat

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-28 03:38 UTC

OP

Is Robotics the next big thing?
Memory and such have already gone up bigly
But I keep seeing news that robotics are going to be the next thing to keep an eye on
What’s /biz/‘s opinion on this, if you agree with the sentiment than what stocks or ETFs should someone look into?
Additionally on the topic of semiconductor ETFs like SOXX or memory ones like DRAM, would you recommend someone goes into those rather than a standard market ETF
I am talking not for short term gains but holding for at least a year or two, something comfortable - are they still a wise decision
I ask cause DRAM is new and who knows how long till keep going up, SOXX has a 25 yeah volatile history and idk what it’ll do in the next years
So basically, is robotics the next big thing and if or if not would you recommend those ETFs and for how long

Early replies (first 3)

62421506
>>62421297
nuclear energy smr is the new meta stocks LEU
SMR
OKLO
RYCEY
CEG
CCJ
BWXT

or etf SMRF

62421522
>>62421297
quantum ai space robot data center ram

62421538
>>62421506
why would this be true

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62421564
>>62421538

''Advanced Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) are a key part of the Department’s goal to develop safe, clean, and affordable nuclear power options. The advanced SMRs currently under development in the United States represent a variety of sizes, technology options, capabilities, and deployment scenarios. These advanced reactors, envisioned to vary in size from tens of megawatts up to hundreds of megawatts, can be used for power generation, process heat, desalination, or other industrial uses. SMR designs may employ light water as a coolant or other non-light water coolants such as a gas, liquid metal, or molten salt''
https://www.energy.gov/ne/advanced-small-modular-reactors-smrs

>Japan is making investments in shipbuilding and fusion energy.

>China is focusing on improving energy resource security and has announced a five-year investment plan of $3 trillion to $3.8 trillion.

>India is looking to diversify its sources of energy imports and use technologies such as coal gasification and biofuels to reduce import dependence.
https://www.morganstanley.com/insights/articles/asia-energy-capex-9t-opportunity-2030

>Hyperscale AI data centers require city-scale electricity loads, making dependable baseload power a strategic necessity.

>Microsoft and Amazon are forming direct nuclear partnerships and pursuing advanced reactor technologies to secure long-term energy supply.

>Energy infrastructure, particularly nuclear generation and uranium supply, is emerging as a structural beneficiary of AI-driven demand growth
https://www.zerohedge.com/ai/ai-boom-powering-nuclear-renaissance

it doesn't matter whether the demand comes from robotics, data centers or space exploration the demand for energy especially clean energy increases every year

62421571
>>62421297

tl;dr: no, because there are no public robotics companies worth buying, and even if you could buy private robotics companies, the ones worth buying are chinese, so no way to buy that anyway.

people have been asking this for a while

at the end of the day, robots require really cheap robotics labor to manufacture/R&D, they don't need battery tech improvements, they don't need AI improvements, they don't need regulation decrease, all they need is just really cheap robotics labor. It requires a ton of iterations to get product market fit on a robot, and that is a function of robotics labor

By an order of magnitude, robotics labor is cheaper in china, and for all intents and purposes, this will stay this way.

so why the fuck would any robotics company want to seriously operate in the US when they can just operate in China?

There's no good answer for this, and since you and I can't buy private chinese companies, the answer to your question is robotics is only the next big thing in abstract, it can't exist in any meaningfully investable way

62421589
>>62421571
What is something se can invest in? I put all my eggs in the Link basket and... Well... Tbh the only reason that investment was successful at all was because I did 6 months of deep research on here with constant reading and asking in threads. Plus I didn't sell in 2021.

But basically I did well because of you guys as a hive mind where ideas and doubts were bashed around. I need your help again

62421677
>>62421564
Cool thanks for the sources

Anyway I thought about it and will just begin a position in DRAM I think

62421711
>>62421571
>because there are no public robotics companies worth buying,
Top KEK!
>>62421589
Ngmi especially if you listen to that retard AND stupid enough to have ever bought l*nk. You're much better off prompting Gemini.

62421737
>>62421297
A High up Microsoft executive told me their next big focus is at home helper robots.
Like a robot to do chores and shit.

I'm assuming AI plays a roll now

item.title: ALTERNATIVE CASH STREAMS / SIDE JOBS / AI GRIFT / SIDE HUSTLES — 17 replies — 4.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
ALTERNATIVE CASH STREAMS / SIDE JOBS / AI GRIFT / SIDE HUSTLES

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-28 00:44 UTC

OP

We all wanna make money on /biz/ right? Instead of chasing returns or making big bets can we discuss actual proven ways to make some money on the side? I have been trying to find easy, not effortless mind you just easy, ways to make money on the side. The total doesn’t have to be big, just consistent. Does anyone have ideas? If it could be something friendly to a noob in that particular field, even better
I know stuff like drop shipping is a fucking scam, flipping Facebook marketplace stuff is not for me
The most common examples you see online are donating plasma and using online survey sites
Does anyone know of anything better, maybe with AI? If so please explain it in detail with examples of how much money you’ve made.
Oh and one more thing YES I HAVE A SALARY JOB AND ALREADY GOT A RAISE FOCUS ON THIS BEING ABOUT SIDE MONEY

Early replies (first 3)

62416647
There still a market for banana-in-your-butt on chaturbate or onlyfans.

Or there's always wendy's

62416661
>>62416647
Ummm how would one make money with Wendy’s silly

62417058
oil is rugging

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62417092
>>62416542
foot modelling

62417962
.

62418176
It's hit and miss and slow, but over the last year I've won $100 playing slot machine apps. I haven't deposited any money in the apps either.

62419720
Bumping

62419807
Mow your neighbors’ lawns you lazy bastard

62420024
>>62419807
theres already companies doing that for citycucks

62420042
>>62416542
what sites even pay for surveys?

62420049
>>62420042
prolific

62420106
>>62419720
https://www.dataannotation.tech/
never tried it, i just found it

62421306
ANYONE

62421335
>>62418176
Yeah? How's that work? They keep a tally of what you owe and you just never pay it if your winnings exceed what you owe? And what apps do you like to use?

62421341
>>62420106
They are fairly selective, but hire people in waves every couple months iirc. I've worked a year on the site and the work has gotten more difficult as AI advances. Requires some focus and brainpower for most tasks nowadays. Less a side hustle and more a part time job imo. If you have specialty knowledge from a degree like stem or law tho could be a great gig until AI is advanced enough to not need you.

62421350
>>62420049
Hate that site. Everything is automated including whatever the hell they use to decide to ban people. Feels like the whole thing is just ran by chatgpt hallucinating everything.

62421354
>>62420106
these things are absolute garbage unless you live in india
